architecture.summary: Latent-GRPO is a reinforcement learning framework that optimizes latent reasoning by compressing intermediate reasoning steps into continuous vector representations (latent tokens) rather than explicit discrete tokens. The system builds on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and Soft-GRPO, adapting them to handle the unique challenges of continuous latent space: it initializes from a Latent-SFT model, applies invalid-sample advantage masking to prevent off-manifold exploration, uses one-sided Gumbel noise sampling to align optimization direction with trajectory advantage, and selects the optimal correct path's first token to avoid harmful mode averaging across multiple valid latent trajectories. Information flows from the LLM's top-k vocabulary distribution (converted to latent embeddings via mixture) through continuous latent reasoning steps, then into explicit discrete token generation for the final answer, with policy optimization applied via a masked PPO-style objective.
trained_components: policy for latent and discrete token generation

biblio.title: Latent-GRPO: Group Relative Policy Optimization for Latent Reasoning

claims.statement: Directly adapting GRPO to latent reasoning is fundamentally non-trivial due to three coupled bottlenecks: absence of intrinsic latent manifolds where unconstrained exploration pushes rollouts off the valid latent manifold, exploration-optimization misalignment where trajectory-level rewards can induce incorrect token-level updates, and latent mixture non-closure where jointly reinforcing multiple correct latent paths can produce an invalid averaged state

claims.metric: Pass@1 improvement and chain length compression ratio
claims.statement: On low-difficulty benchmarks, Latent-GRPO improves over its latent initialization by 7.86 Pass@1 points on average while using 4.44× shorter reasoning chains than explicit GRPO

claims.metric: Pass@1 improvement over Latent-SFT; Pass@1 advantage over GRPO; chain length compression ratio
claims.statement: On high-difficulty benchmarks, Latent-GRPO improves over its latent initialization by 14.77 Pass@1 points on average, outperforms explicit GRPO by 4.27 points, and uses 3.31× shorter reasoning chains
On GSM8K-Aug (low-difficulty), Latent-GRPO achieves Pass@1 of 66.29 with reasoning length 23.80, improving over Latent-SFT (47.55, 22.00) and surpassing explicit GRPO (62.26, 111.1)
On Math500 (high-difficulty), Latent-GRPO achieves Pass@1 of 80.40 with reasoning length 929, improving over Latent-SFT (64.85, 1070) and surpassing explicit GRPO (75.15, 2982)
Under Gumbel sampling at pass@k on AIME24, Latent-GRPO reaches 46.7 (pass@8) and 50.0 (pass@16), substantially outperforming explicit GRPO which reaches 23.3 (pass@16)

One-sided Noise Sampling is critical: removing this design causes validation performance to drop sharply and eventually collapse, with response length becoming much less controlled on both GSM8K-Aug and Math500

First Token Selection provides additional improvement beyond One-sided Noise Sampling, with milder degradation when removed on low-difficulty tasks but pronounced instability on high-difficulty reasoning problems
